top_stories.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
top_stories.summary: The article examines persistent OAuth tokens granted to third-party AI tools and productivity apps connected to Google and Microsoft as a largely unmonitored attack vector. These tokens have no expiration dates, bypass MFA and perimeter controls, and allow attackers to maintain access without credentials.

top_stories.title: Omada Launches Omada Identity Cloud Private for Regulated Enterprises and Government Organizations
top_stories.summary: Omada Identity has launched "Omada Identity Cloud Private," a new deployment option that runs the full Omada Identity Cloud platform within the customer's own tenant. The offering is targeted at regulated enterprises and government organizations requiring strict data boundaries and auditability for compliance purposes.

top_stories.title: Attacks Abuse Windows Phone Link to Steal Texts & Bypass 2FA
top_stories.summary: Attackers are using the CloudZ RAT and a new plugin called Pheno to abuse Windows Phone Link, a feature that bridges PCs and smartphones, to steal SMS messages. This enables threat actors to intercept SMS-based 2FA codes, effectively bypassing multi-factor authentication controls.

top_stories.title: CISA gives feds four days to patch Ivanti flaw exploited as zero-day
top_stories.summary: CISA has mandated U.S. federal agencies patch a high-severity zero-day vulnerability in Ivanti Endpoint Manager Mobile (EPMM) within four days. The flaw is actively being exploited in zero-day attacks, triggering an emergency directive for federal network security.

top_stories.title: New Linux PamDOORa Backdoor Uses PAM Modules to Steal SSH Credentials
top_stories.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a new Linux backdoor called PamDOORa, advertised on a Russian cybercrime forum for $1,600, which abuses Pluggable Authentication Modules (PAM) to steal SSH credentials. The toolkit enables persistent SSH access through a hardcoded magic password and specific TCP port combination, representing a direct attack vector against Linux authentication infrastructure.

news.title: Helping North Korean IT remote workers is becoming a fast track to prison
news.summary: Two U.S. nationals were sentenced to 18 months in prison for running "laptop farm" operations that helped North Korean IT workers fraudulently gain employment at nearly 70 American companies, generating over $1.2 million for Pyongyang. The cases highlight identity fraud vulnerabilities in remote workforce verification and hiring processes, where fake identities were used to bypass employment screening at U.S. organizations.

news.title: A new frontier: Identity stack evolves for agentic systems
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how the identity stack is evolving to support agentic AI systems. The article explores identity's transformation into a real-time control plane as AI agents require new approaches to authentication, authorization, and access management.

news.title: Canvas login portals hacked in mass ShinyHunters extortion campaign
news.summary: The ShinyHunters extortion gang breached Instructure and defaced Canvas login portals for hundreds of colleges and universities by exploiting a vulnerability. The attack targeted authentication login portals used by educational institutions to access the Canvas learning management system.

news.title: PCPJack Credential Stealer Exploits 5 CVEs to Spread Worm-Like Across Cloud Systems
news.summary: Researchers have disclosed PCPJack, a credential theft framework that targets exposed cloud infrastructure by exploiting five CVEs to spread worm-like across systems. The toolset harvests credentials from cloud, container, developer, productivity, and financial services, then exfiltrates the stolen data through attacker-controlled infrastructure.

news.title: World Passkey Day: Advancing passwordless authentication
news.summary: Microsoft published a post on World Passkey Day outlining its efforts to advance passkey adoption as a replacement for passwords. The initiative focuses on reducing phishing risk and enabling simpler, more secure sign-ins through passwordless authentication.

news.title: 5 leading enterprise password managers to consider
news.summary: The article outlines key features enterprise password managers should provide for administrators. It covers available options and required capabilities for enterprise-grade password management solutions.

news.title: Semperis Names John Podboy Chief Information Security Officer
news.summary: Semperis has appointed John Podboy as its new Chief Information Security Officer. Podboy will focus on risk, resilience, compliance, and agentic AI-driven cyber defense as the company enters its next growth phase.

news.title: Your AI Agents Are Already Inside the Perimeter. Do You Know What They're Doing?
news.summary: Gartner has released its inaugural Market Guide for Guardian Agents, addressing the governance gap created by rapid enterprise AI agent deployment. The report highlights that AI agent adoption is outpacing governance policy controls, raising concerns for identity security teams managing non-human identities and access.

news.title: Windows Phone Link Exploited by CloudZ RAT to Steal Credentials and OTPs
news.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have detailed an attack using CloudZ RAT and a previously undocumented plugin called Pheno that exploits Windows Phone Link to steal credentials and one-time passwords (OTPs). The malware targets authentication credentials and OTPs, directly undermining MFA mechanisms used to protect identity and access.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server is now generally available
news.summary: AWS has announced the general availability of the AWS MCP Server, a managed remote Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI agents and coding assistants with authenticated access to AWS services. The server is part of AWS's Agent Toolkit and uses authentication mechanisms to control AI agent access to AWS resources.

news.title: Keeper Security Research Reveals 89% of IT Leaders Struggle to Manage Growing Identity Footprint Amid AI Expansion
news.summary: Keeper Security released a global study of 3,200 cybersecurity decision-makers finding that 89% of IT leaders struggle to manage growing identity footprints amid AI expansion. The research found 96% of respondents report disconnected identity tools are creating exploitable security gaps, with AI adoption cited as a key driver of identity sprawl.

news.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
news.summary: A proof-of-concept exploit demonstrates that Microsoft Edge stores passwords in process memory, allowing an attacker with admin privileges to extract stored credentials. The vulnerability poses an enterprise risk as stolen passwords could be used for further malicious activity and credential-based attacks.

news.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
news.summary: A critical authentication-bypass vulnerability in cPanel has been disclosed, with multiple proof-of-concept exploits appearing shortly after. A researcher claims zero-day exploitation activity has been occurring for at least a month prior to disclosure.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. His admitted crimes involved SMS phishing attacks in 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in cryptocurrency theft from investors.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence-linked hackers are exploiting vulnerabilities in older routers to mass harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens across more than 18,000 networks. The campaign operates without deploying malware, instead silently siphoning authentication tokens to gain unauthorized access to user accounts.
